title: Geirangerfjord & Waterfalls
blurb: A week inside Norway's most iconic UNESCO fjord — guided ridge walks, cold-water swims and quiet evenings under the midnight sun.
longDescription:
Geirangerfjord is where Norway shows off — sheer cliffs, ribboned waterfalls and water so still it mirrors the sky. Over eight days we move through it slowly: guided ridge walks by day, cold-water dips when the mood takes us, and long light evenings with nothing to rush towards.

Groups stay small so the pace can flex to the people in it. No expedition experience is needed — just a reasonable level of fitness and a willingness to be outdoors in all weather.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.title: Arrival & settling in
itinerary.text: Meet in Ålesund, transfer to the fjord, and an easy first walk to find our feet.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.title: Skageflå farm trail
itinerary.text: A classic climb to the abandoned mountain farm with views straight down the fjord.

itinerary.label: Days 3–4
itinerary.title: Ridge walks & waterfalls
itinerary.text: Two days on the high paths above the fjord, with waterfall swims along the way.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.title: Rest & sauna
itinerary.text: A gentler day — journaling, a wood-fired sauna, and an optional kayak.

itinerary.label: Days 6–7
itinerary.title: Summit days
itinerary.text: Our biggest objective, paced for presence, with a celebration at the top.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.text: A slow morning and transfer back.

title: Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock)
blurb: The classic climb to the 604-metre cliff above Lysefjord, plus mindful forest walks and a grounding breath-work session at the summit.
longDescription:
The Pulpit Rock is the postcard — a flat shelf of stone 604 metres above Lysefjord. But the days around it are the real journey: quiet forest paths, breath-work at altitude, and time to notice what the mountains bring up.

A focused long-weekend reset, suitable for confident walkers.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.text: Gather near Stavanger and walk into the forest to our base.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.title: Preikestolen
itinerary.text: The main event — an early start to reach the cliff before the crowds.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.title: Forest & fjord
itinerary.text: Mindful walking, a fjord swim, and an afternoon breath-work session.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.title: Free ascent
itinerary.text: Choose your own pace on a quieter ridge route.

title: Lofoten Islands Retreat
blurb: Jagged peaks straight out of the sea, white beaches and Arctic light. A small-group reset for body and mind.
longDescription:
Lofoten barely feels real — granite peaks rising straight out of a turquoise sea, white-sand beaches, and in summer a sun that never quite sets. Six days of island hopping, ridge walks and Arctic swims, with plenty of room to simply be.

Dates for 2026 are being finalised — join the list to hear first.

itinerary.label: Days 1–2
itinerary.title: Reine & Reinebringen
itinerary.text: Arrive and warm up on the islands' most famous viewpoint.

itinerary.label: Days 3–4
itinerary.title: Beaches & ridges
itinerary.text: Kvalvika beach, Ryten, and quieter trails away from the crowds.

itinerary.label: Days 5–6
itinerary.title: Open water & farewell
itinerary.text: An Arctic swim, a final summit, and a slow goodbye.

title: Mont Blanc · Chamonix
blurb: Alpine meadows, glacier views and high mountain air. Daily guided hikes paced for presence, not performance.
longDescription:
Beneath Mont Blanc, the trails run through alpine meadows, past glaciers and up to balconies with the whole massif laid out in front of you. Seven days of guided hiking at a pace built for presence rather than peak-bagging.

2026 dates to be confirmed.

itinerary.label: Days 1–2
itinerary.title: Arrival & acclimatising
itinerary.text: Settle into Chamonix and ease in with valley balcony trails.

itinerary.label: Days 3–5
itinerary.title: The high balconies
itinerary.text: Grand Balcon walks, glacier viewpoints and mountain refuges.

itinerary.label: Days 6–7
itinerary.title: Summit day & farewell
itinerary.text: A bigger objective, then a final evening together.

title: Free intro call
blurb: A relaxed chat to see if working together feels right and to find the best starting point for you.

title: Nature-assisted therapy
blurb: A guided 1:1 session outdoors — movement, body awareness and talk in a calm natural setting.

title: Psychomotor therapy
blurb: Body-oriented therapy to ease tension, settle the nervous system and reconnect with yourself.

title: 5-session package
blurb: A discounted series of five sessions for deeper, ongoing work — booked at a pace that suits you.
